skill.name: happyhorse-1-0
skill.description: Generate text-to-video with HappyHorse 1.0 on RunComfy. Documents HappyHorse 1.0's strengths (#1 on Artificial Analysis Video Arena, native 1080p with in-pass synchronized audio, multi-shot character consistency, 6-language prompt support), the duration / aspect-ratio / resolution schema, and when to route to Wan 2.7 / Seedance 2 / LTX 2 instead. Calls `runcomfy run happyhorse/happyhorse-1-0/text-to-video` through the local RunComfy CLI. Triggers on "happyhorse", "happy horse", "happyhorse 1.0", "happyhorse video", or any explicit ask to generate video with this model.
skill.summary: Generate high-quality 1080p text-to-video with synchronized audio via HappyHorse 1.0 on RunComfy
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged 86 potential issues, but evaluation reveals most are false positives. The skill is a legitimate RunComfy CLI wrapper for video generation. Backtick detections are markdown code formatting, not Ruby execution. Network calls go to documented RunComfy API endpoints. Filesystem access is limited to output directory. Security documentation is present and comprehensive.

low_findings.title: Token Storage in User Home Directory
low_findings.description: The RunComfy CLI stores the API token at ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json. While this is standard CLI practice and the file is created with mode 0600 (owner-only), accessing the user's home directory is a filesystem risk factor.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Token storage location is documented with proper permission settings. This is standard CLI practice, not a vulnerability.

content.value_statement: Creating professional videos with AI requires navigating complex APIs and settings. This skill provides a ready-to-use integration with HappyHorse 1.0 (ranked #1 on Artificial Analysis Video Arena), handling CLI commands, parameter validation, and output management so you can focus on prompt crafting.
actual_capabilities: Generate 1080p text-to-video clips up to 15 seconds using HappyHorse 1.0
Create multi-shot videos with character consistency across shots
Produce videos with synchronized audio (dialogue, ambient, Foley) in a single pass
Support 6 prompt languages: English, Chinese, Japanese, Korean, German, French
Auto-route to alternative models (Wan 2.7, Seedance 2, LTX 2) when appropriate
limitations: Maximum video duration is 15 seconds per clip
Cannot pass external audio for lip-sync (use Wan 2.7 for audio-driven video)
No free image-to-video on this template (text-to-video only)
Only 5 aspect ratio options; ultra-wide formats may be cropped

use_cases.title: Multilingual Social Media Ads
use_cases.description: Create platform-native video ads in multiple languages with consistent branding and character appearance across a campaign.
use_cases.target_user: Marketing teams and social media managers

use_cases.title: Cinematic Explainers with Voiceover
use_cases.description: Produce talking-head or location-based explainer videos with synchronized ambient audio and dialogue in a single generation pass.

use_cases.title: Multi-Shot Brand Storytelling
use_cases.description: Generate coherent brand narratives with consistent characters across multiple scenes without manual frame-by-frame editing.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Video Generation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use HappyHorse 1.0 to generate a 5-second video with this prompt: [DESCRIBE_SCENE_AND_ACTION]
prompt_templates.scenario: Basic text-to-video generation with default settings

prompt_templates.title: Vertical Short for Social
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a 9:16 vertical video, 8 seconds, no watermark. Prompt: [YOUR_PROMPT_WITH_CAMERA_AND_MOTION]
prompt_templates.scenario: Creating platform-native content for TikTok, Reels, or Shorts

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Shot Consistency
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a two-shot sequence with character [CHARACTER_DESCRIPTION]. Shot 1: [BEAT_1]. Shot 2: Same character, [BEAT_2].
prompt_templates.scenario: Producing coherent multi-scene narratives with consistent characters

prompt_templates.title: Cinematic Quality Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a cinematic 1080p video. Front-load the shot type (wide, tracking, locked tripod), specify lens feel (35mm anamorphic, shallow DOF), and describe motion over time. Include audio direction. Prompt: [DETAILED_CINEMATIC_DESCRIPTION]
prompt_templates.scenario: Achieving broadcast-quality output with specific visual language

output_examples.input: Generate a video of an astronaut skiing on the moon with Earth in the background
output_examples.output: A 5-second 1080p video clip showing a lone astronaut in dusty orange suit skiing across a lunar plain, leaving parallel tracks in gray regolith. Mid-stride, poles planted, with fine dust haze and crescent Earth visible above the horizon.

output_examples.input: Create a vertical short of a barista making espresso
output_examples.output: A 9:16 vertical video of a barista in black apron pulling a single espresso shot with steam rising into morning sun. Close-up handheld, shallow depth of field, warm cafe ambience.

best_practices: Describe motion over time with temporal verbs: use "walks two paces" not "walking" for clear movement
Front-load camera directions: start with shot type (wide, tracking, close-up) before describing subject
Restate character anchors in each shot of multi-shot sequences to maintain consistency
anti_patterns: Static-frame descriptions with no temporal verbs result in vague, unfocused motion
Piling multiple actions into one prompt ("walks AND dog runs AND car passes") dilutes each
Prompts exceeding 2500 characters degrade quality; keep descriptions focused

faq.question: What video quality does HappyHorse 1.0 produce?
faq.answer: HappyHorse 1.0 generates native 1080p video. It ranks #1 on the Artificial Analysis Video Arena with an Elo of 1333 for text-to-video.

faq.question: Can I add my own audio track for lip-sync?
faq.answer: This template is text-to-video only. For audio-driven video with lip-sync, use Wan 2.7 which accepts an audio_url parameter.

faq.question: How long can the generated videos be?
faq.answer: Videos can be 3 to 15 seconds. For longer narratives, create multiple clips and stitch them together in post.

faq.question: Does this skill work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill is designed for all three AI coding tools and uses the local RunComfy CLI for video generation.

faq.question: How is my API token stored securely?
faq.answer: The RunComfy CLI stores your token in ~/.config/runcomfy/token.json with 0600 permissions (owner-only access). Set RUNCOMFY_TOKEN environment variable in CI environments.
